fix: Read the active workspace (useSearchParams().get('workspace') or the workspace store) on the page, filter prospects to that workspace's leads before computing estimatedProspects and before passing into selectProspects(). Tag ProspectLead with workspaceId (person.workspaceId already exists in schema) and filter on it; show 'semua workspace' only for managers/superadmin.

issue: Run history (GET /api/db/autopilot-runs) returns the last 50 runs for the whole tenant with no workspace filter, and autopilot_runs has no workspaceId column at all (schema lib/db/schema.ts:143-153). The 'Riwayat run' card therefore mixes runs from every workspace/rep in the tenant, and there is no way to scope or even attribute a run to the rep/workspace that launched it.
location: app/api/db/autopilot-runs/route.ts:24-30 (select … orderBy … limit(50), no workspace/owner filter); lib/db/schema.ts:143-153 (autopilotRunsTable has no workspaceId / ownerUserId); lib/stores/autopilot-store.ts:227-242 (merge by id only)
realWorld: A rep opening Autopilot inside their workspace expects to see THEIR past runs, not the whole company's. With per-rep attribution being a core principle (MEMORY: execution + attribution per individual sales rep's account), an unscoped tenant-wide history is both a privacy/scoping leak and confusing — a count/list over the wrong set, exactly like the contacts count-badge bug.
fix: Add workspaceId + ownerUserId columns to autopilot_runs; set them in the PUT from the active workspace/session; filter the GET by workspaceId (when provided) and by ownerUserId for non-managers. In the store, hydrate per-workspace.

issue: metrics.prospectsEngaged is bumped by selected.length at audience-selection time (before any outreach), so 'prospek engaged' counts everyone the run TARGETED, not everyone it actually contacted. liSent is the real 'engaged' number. The Riwayat-run row and RunSummary present prospectsEngaged as if it were an outcome.
location: lib/autopilot/orchestrator.ts:242 (store().bumpMetric('prospectsEngaged', selected.length)); app/(app)/autopilot/page.tsx:264-267 (HistoryRow shows run.metrics.prospectsEngaged with a Users icon)
realWorld: In a real funnel, 'engaged' means a request actually went out (or a reply happened). Counting the selected audience as 'engaged' overstates activity — if LinkedIn throttles/blocks sends, prospectsEngaged still shows the full target count. The number should reflect sent/accepted, not selected.
fix: Either rename the metric/label to 'Prospek terpilih' for prospectsEngaged, or bump it from the send-li-requests loop on a successful 'sent' result (mirroring liSent), so 'engaged' reflects real outreach.

issue: The field page shows ALL field reps (useFieldReps → entire field-reps.json) and the visits log shows ALL visits (useVisits → entire visits.json) with no scoping to the logged-in user, tenant, or team. A Sales Rep sees every other rep's live location, visit counts, and visit outcomes.
location: app/(app)/field/page.tsx:30,34-37; app/(app)/field/visits/page.tsx:31,66; lib/api-mock/hooks.ts:120-126 (useFieldReps/useVisits — no tenant/owner param); lib/api-mock/data.ts:75-76
realWorld: Per doc 41 per-rep isolation, a rep should see only their own route/visits; a manager sees their team; superadmin sees all. Showing the whole company's field movements to every rep is both a scoping leak and not how an Indonesian field-sales org operates (a rep tracks their own kunjungan, a Kepala Cabang tracks their cabang's team).
fix: Scope useFieldReps/useVisits by the session's role: Sales Rep → own id, Manager → team member ids, Superadmin → all. Add a role/owner filter to the mock hooks (and the eventual DB query), defaulting to the current user.

issue: Selecting a rep on the 'Semua' tab then switching to 'Live' filters the list to status==='kunjungan' but does NOT clear selectedId. The detail panel at the bottom and the map still render the now-hidden selected rep (selected = reps.find(...), not list.find(...)), so the sidebar shows no highlighted row yet a detail card + route polyline for a rep that isn't in the visible list.
location: app/(app)/field/page.tsx:34-37 (list filtered by tab; selected resolved from full reps, not list), 104-117 (detail panel uses selected), 121 (FieldMap reps={list} but selectedId still points at a non-list rep)
realWorld: Switching the Live filter and still seeing a selected rep who is on 'istirahat'/'selesai' (not live) is incoherent — the filter visibly fails to affect the selection state. A user expects the filter to either keep the rep visible or drop the selection.
fix: When tab changes, reset selectedId if the selected rep is no longer in list (useEffect on [tab] that clears selection when !list.some(r=>r.id===selectedId)), or resolve selected from list instead of reps so the detail/map stay consistent with the filter.

issue: kpi.activeCustomers (351, labeled 'terdaftar di seluruh alur') and per-flow flow.enrolled are seeded independently and never reconcile. Enrolling a candidate bumps the recommended flow's enrolled (+1) but never touches kpi.activeCustomers, and the sum of all flow.enrolled (142+38+21+86+64=351 by coincidence of seed) drifts the moment you enroll. The headline 'pelanggan aktif retensi' is a static number that contradicts the live flow counts after any action.
location: lib/api-mock/retention.ts:204-212 (seedKpi.activeCustomers=351); lib/stores/retention-store.ts:133-142 (enrollCandidate bumps flow.enrolled only, never kpi); app/(app)/retention/page.tsx:126-129 (KPI tile shows kpi.activeCustomers 'terdaftar di seluruh alur')
realWorld: A manager reads 'pelanggan aktif retensi: 351 — terdaftar di seluruh alur' as the live sum of enrollments. After daftarkan-ing a kandidat the per-flow count goes up but the headline stays 351, so the two numbers openly disagree — a count over the wrong (static) set, like the contacts count-badge bug.
fix: Derive kpi.activeCustomers from the flows (sum of flow.enrolled) instead of a frozen seed value, OR increment kpi.activeCustomers inside enrollCandidate. Make the headline a computed selector so it tracks the same source as the per-flow counts.

issue: Saving the audience filter does nothing except store segment/min/max/tags for the flow; there is no action anywhere that enrolls the filtered audience into the flow. The 'Catatan AI' panel claims 'Filter di atas akan menentukan pelanggan mana yang diikutsertakan' and the save toast says 'X pelanggan memenuhi', but no customer is ever included as a result of saving — enrollment only happens one-by-one via the dashboard CandidateTable's Daftarkan button.
location: app/(app)/retention/[flowId]/page.tsx:310-338 (Catatan AI copy + estimate); components/retention/audience-filter.tsx:70-78 (onSaveFilter only setAudienceFilter); lib/stores/retention-store.ts:144-147 (setAudienceFilter just writes the map)
realWorld: A marketer sets a segment + day-range filter expecting 'apply this audience to the flow' (bulk enroll). Here Simpan filter is a dead end — it changes an estimate number and nothing else. The promised cause→effect (filter → diikutsertakan) never happens.
fix: Add an 'Daftarkan audiens ini' action that enrolls all candidates matching the saved filter into the flow (bump flow.enrolled by the matched count and remove them from candidates), or relabel the panel/toast to make clear the filter is only a preview/targeting note with no enrollment side effect.

issue: estimateAudience ignores segment AND tags — it filters candidates by daysSincePurchase only. The save toast 'Filter audiens disimpan — X pelanggan memenuhi' and both 'Estimasi audiens' panels present X as if it honored the chosen Segmen (UMKM/Korporat/etc.) and Tag (VIP/Repeat/…). Changing segment or tags never changes the number.
location: lib/api-mock/retention.ts:296-305 (estimateAudience uses only min/max days); components/retention/audience-filter.tsx:52-59,77 (estimate + toast); app/(app)/retention/[flowId]/page.tsx:331 (~estimateAudience(candidates, audienceFilter))
realWorld: Although a small footnote admits 'Segmen & tag bersifat pratinjau', the prominent estimate + the save toast still read as a real audience size. A rep tightening to 'Segmen: Korporat, Tag: VIP' and seeing the same count will (correctly) distrust the whole estimate. Segmentation that doesn't reflect the chosen criteria is the core retention targeting feature being non-functional.
fix: Give candidates segment/tag attributes (or join to person/company) and filter on them in estimateAudience; until then, make the estimate visually 'preview only' and word the toast as 'rentang hari saja' so it doesn't claim segment/tag were applied.

issue: Retention candidates are built from seedContacts.slice(0,14).slice(0,8) — a fixed first-8 of the GLOBAL contact seed — with no tenant or workspace scoping, and daysSincePurchase/recommendedFlow are synthesized from a hash, not from any real purchase/deal data. The 'X kandidat' badge and 'Kandidat siap didaftarkan' table therefore show the same 8 cross-tenant contacts for everyone.
realWorld: Retention candidates should be real post-purchase customers of THIS tenant/workspace, derived from closed deals/orders, not the first 8 rows of a shared contacts fixture. Like the contacts cross-workspace mixing, the candidate list isn't scoped, and the AI-note/flow recommendation is hash-noise, not a real fit signal.
fix: Build candidates from won deals/orders for the active tenant (and workspace if scoped), compute daysSincePurchase from the real last-purchase date, and pick recommendedFlow from actual segment/recency rather than i % FLOW_POOL.length.

issue: Type filter chips filter visibleFlows but the 'activeFlowCount' badge in the header ('N alur aktif') and the KPI tiles always count/aggregate over the unfiltered flows. After filtering to e.g. 'Upsell' the grid shows 1 flow while the header still says '4 alur aktif', so the badge doesn't reflect the visible set.
location: app/(app)/retention/page.tsx:42-44 (visibleFlows filtered, activeFlowCount over all flows), 56-58 (badge), 197-201 (grid renders visibleFlows)
realWorld: Minor, but it's the same pattern as the confirmed count-badge bug: a count shown next to a filtered list that ignores the filter. A user filtering to Upsell expects the active-count context to follow the filter (or the badge to clearly be a global total).
fix: Either compute activeFlowCount from visibleFlows (so it tracks the filter) or label it explicitly as a global total ('N alur aktif total') so it's not read as the count of what's on screen.

issue: The flow detail Save button persists name/description/triggerCondition to the store, but the toast 'Alur "X" disimpan' implies durable persistence while the retention store is localStorage-only (persist middleware) and nothing is written to a backend. Trigger condition is free-text with no validation/structure, so it can never actually drive enrollment.
location: app/(app)/retention/[flowId]/page.tsx:103-110 (onSave + toast); lib/stores/retention-store.ts:54-56,160-169 (persist to localStorage 'maira-retention-v1' only); flow.triggerCondition is a plain string
realWorld: A real after-sales flow's trigger ('30 hari sejak pembelian terakhir') must be a structured, evaluable rule that actually enrolls customers. Here it's prose saved to the browser only — editing it changes a label, not behavior, and 'disimpan' overstates durability for a multi-user tool.
fix: Model triggerCondition as a structured rule (type + threshold + segment) that a job can evaluate, persist flows to the DB (cadences/flow tables) so edits survive across devices/users, and word the toast to match what's actually saved.

issue: The 'Funnel per channel' and 'Channel quick stats' silently DROP every deal whose sourceChannel isn't one of whatsapp/email/instagram/tokopedia, while the headline KPIs (Pendapatan MTD, Deal ditutup MTD, Tingkat konversi) count ALL deals. In the seed, deals are tokopedia 5, linkedin 11, sms 9, email 2, shopee 9, whatsapp 4, instagram 10 — so 29 of 50 deals (58%) vanish from the funnel/per-channel cards but still inflate revenue and conversion. The funnel total will never reconcile with the KPI strip.
location: app/(app)/reports/page.tsx:247-261 (const label = CHANNEL_LABEL[raw] ?? null; if (!label) continue;)
realWorld: A sales manager reading this report sees 'Pendapatan MTD' from 50 deals but a funnel built from only 21. linkedin and shopee are first-class channels in this market (the dashboard itself lists Tokopedia; shopee/linkedin/sms deals are real seed rows), yet they're erased from channel performance. The numbers literally don't add up, which destroys trust in the whole report.
fix: Add 'linkedin', 'shopee', 'sms' (and a 'Lainnya' bucket for anything unmapped) to CHANNEL_LABEL/channelOrder/CHANNEL_ACCENT so the funnel covers every deal, OR explicitly compute the KPIs from the same channel-filtered set the funnel uses and label the funnel 'channel utama' honestly. Don't `continue` rows into oblivion.

issue: The channel quick-filter only offers all/whatsapp/email/instagram/tokopedia. Deals (and conversations/tasks/activity) on linkedin, sms, and shopee — 29 of 50 seeded deals — can never be filtered to and are invisibly folded into the 'Semua' totals with no chip to surface them. There's no 'Lainnya' option, so the user has no way to see or reconcile those deals.
realWorld: An Indonesian B2B rep working LinkedIn outreach or a B2C rep on Shopee literally cannot view their own pipeline from the dashboard's channel lens — the majority channel by deal count (linkedin) isn't even a tab. The filter set is hardcoded to four channels that don't match the actual data the seed/RPA produces.
fix: Derive CHANNEL_FILTERS from the distinct sourceChannel values present in the data (plus an 'all'), or add linkedin/shopee/sms chips and a 'Lainnya' catch-all so every deal is reachable and the per-channel slices sum back to 'Semua'.

issue: 'Deal tanpa nilai', 'Deal tanpa kontak', 'Kontak tanpa email/telepon' are computed from fields that are never empty in the data: every seeded deal has value>0, contactId, owner, expectedClose (verified: 0/50 missing each). So noValue=0, noContact=0 always, and cleanRate would be 100% — except 'Deal stagnan > 30 hari' is faked with `Math.max(stale, Math.min(8, hashStr('stale-'+totalDeals) % 14))`, injecting a non-existent count. The 'X masalah' badge and severity chips are therefore fabricated, not measured.
realWorld: A data-quality screen whose counts are a hash of the dataset size, not the actual dirty rows, will send a manager to 'Tinjau' a list of 8 stagnant deals that don't exist. Real pipeline hygiene must reflect real missing fields; here it's theater.
fix: Drop the hashStr fallback for stagnant; report the true `stale` count. If the demo seed has no dirty rows, either seed some (a few deals with null value/contactId, contacts with null email/phone) or label the section honestly as '0 masalah — data bersih' instead of manufacturing counts.

issue: Deal attribution joins members (from DB, identified by userId) to deals by free-text owner NAME (`dealsByName.get(m.name.toLowerCase())`), while leads join by userId (personTable.assignedTo). deals.owner is free text ('Andi Hidayat', 'Maya Kusuma', 'Teguh Saputra', 'Rina Permata' in seed) with no FK to users. Any name mismatch (typo, married name, 'Andi H.', two reps named Budi) silently yields 0 deals/closing for that rep, or merges two people. There's also no workspace/tenant scoping of deals beyond RLS — deals have no assignedTo column at all.
location: app/api/team/monitoring/route.ts:50-62 (dealsByName keyed by owner name) + :66-67 (dl = dealsByName.get(m.name.toLowerCase()))
realWorld: Monitoring is the manager's source of truth for 'closing dari siapa'. Attributing money by display-name string is how a rep gets credited 0 closings (or another rep's closings) the moment names don't match exactly — a guaranteed dispute in any real Indonesian sales team where names are non-unique and free-typed.
fix: Attribute deals by a stable ownerUserId FK, not name. Add deals.assignedTo/ownerUserId (the person/quote tables already use assignedTo/ownerUserId) and key dealsByUser on userId. Until the schema has it, at minimum normalize+warn on unmatched owner strings rather than silently zeroing.

issue: Every KPI delta is a hardcoded string unrelated to the data: revenue '+18,2%' / 'vs. bulan lalu', deals '+4 deal', conversion '+2,1 pp', cycle '-3 hari'. avgCycleDays is `22 + (hashStr(...) % 14)` — a hash of dataset size, not an actual cycle time (deals have createdAt + expectedClose, so a real cycle is computable). The page presents these as live analytics under a 'Diperbarui …' timestamp.
realWorld: A manager will quote '+18,2% vs bulan lalu' in a board update — a number that is literally a constant in the source. The 'Data demo' badge mitigates this, but the deltas sit on tiles next to genuinely-derived values (revenue from real deals), so the mix is deceptive. avgCycle can and should be real.
fix: Compute avgCycleDays from closed deals' (expectedClose - createdAt) average. Either compute real period-over-period deltas (needs a prior window) or remove the delta badges. Don't render fabricated deltas beside derived KPIs.

issue: 'Closing minggu ini' filters `t <= WEEK_AHEAD` with NO lower bound, so any open (non-tutup) deal whose expectedClose is in the PAST (overdue) is counted as 'closing this week'. With the pinned NOW=2026-05-25 the seed happens to have 0 overdue, so it's latent, but the logic is wrong and will misreport the moment data has an overdue open deal.
location: app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx:122-126 (closing filter) and the same in lib/api-mock/hooks.ts:195-197 (useDashboard)
realWorld: Overdue deals are not 'closing this week' — they're slipping and need escalation, the opposite signal. A rep relies on this tile to plan the week; counting stale overdue deals as imminent wins is exactly the kind of 'gak sesuai lapangan' miscount that hides risk.
fix: Bound the window on both sides: `t >= NOW && t <= WEEK_AHEAD`. Surface overdue/open deals separately (e.g. a 'terlambat' count) rather than folding them into closing-this-week.

issue: When the DB is not configured (the prototype's default — `!hasDb()`), the monitoring route returns `{ data: [] }`, so the page renders an empty roster and the summary tiles show 'Total lead 0 / Total closing 0 / Nilai closing Rp 0'. Unlike contacts/conversations routes which fall back to seed fixtures, team monitoring has no mock fallback, so the headline manager screen looks broken/empty in the standard demo.
realWorld: The whole point of this screen (doc 41) is to let a manager see who's active and who's closing. In a no-DB demo it shows zeros — a manager would conclude the team did nothing. Every other list in the app degrades to seed data; this one doesn't.
fix: Provide a mock roster fallback (derive reps from the seed deals' distinct owners + a fieldReps fixture, aggregate leads/closings from seed deals/contacts) so the page is populated in mock mode, mirroring the contacts/conversations fallback pattern.

issue: Reports is visible to Sales Reps (member) — it is NOT managerOnly in side-nav (unlike /team) — yet the Sales leaderboard, per-rep revenue, and full pipeline are computed from ALL tenant deals with no per-rep restriction. deriveSales groups every closed deal by owner and shows the top-5 reps' names and money to any rep. doc 41 explicitly says members are 'scoped to their own assigned leads'.
location: app/(app)/reports/page.tsx:285-299 (leaderboard over all closed deals) + components/layout/side-nav.tsx:121 (/reports has no managerOnly)
realWorld: Per doc 41 per-rep isolation, a Sales Rep should not see the whole team's closings and rankings (a competitive/privacy concern in real sales orgs). Here any rep opens Laporan and sees every colleague's revenue and the leaderboard. Either gate the rep-comparison sections to managers, or filter the report to the rep's own deals.
fix: Either mark the leaderboard + cross-rep sections managerOnly (gate via role from session like /team does), or filter deriveSales inputs to the current user's owned deals for non-managers. Match the isolation model already enforced on /team.

issue: The Akurasi AI tab redefines 'AI error' as 'an autopilot step that fell back to the mock/template provider' (e.source === 'mock'). This conflates 'Deepseek was unreachable so we used a template' with 'the AI gave a wrong answer'. When there's no autopilot history it shows a static 3.79% from fixtures. So the 'Tingkat kesalahan AI' headline is really a provider-availability rate, not an accuracy/error rate.
realWorld: A manager reads 'Tingkat kesalahan AI 3.79%' as 'AI got it wrong 3.79% of the time' and may pause automation. In reality it can be 100% 'errors' simply because no API key is set (mock provider) — the offline demo's normal state. This mislabels infra fallback as quality failure, the inverse of the static fixture's meaning ('Salah informasi produk', 'Harga tidak akurat').
fix: Rename the metric to 'AI fallback rate' / 'Tingkat fallback template' when derived from source==='mock', or actually track flagged/incorrect responses (the auto_reply_event/escalation tables carry confidence + decision) and compute accuracy from human-reviewed flags. Don't equate 'template used' with 'wrong answer'.

issue: Response rate is defined as conversations with unread===0 over total conversations ('replied = unread 0'). A brand-new inbound lead that no one has opened/replied to also has unread>0 (correctly unanswered), but a conversation we never replied to where the customer's last message was read (unread 0) counts as 'replied'. unread is a read-receipt counter, not a reply indicator, so responseRate measures 'inbox zero' not 'we responded'.
realWorld: A rep can mark everything read without replying and the dashboard reports a high 'Respon WhatsApp %'. Real response-rate is outbound-reply / inbound-threads. Using unread-count as a proxy rewards clearing badges, not answering customers — a classic vanity-metric that doesn't match how a CS/sales rep is actually measured.
fix: Compute response rate from messages: share of inbound threads that have at least one outbound message after the latest inbound (the messages table has direction in/out). Failing that, rename the tile to 'Inbox terbaca' so it's honest about counting unread===0, not replies.

issue: Leads-owned counts persons by assignedTo across the whole tenant with no workspace dimension, even though person rows carry workspaceId (doc 44) and the rest of the app scopes leads per workspace. A rep working two workspaces has all their leads summed into one number with no breakdown, and a deal/lead reassigned to a different workspace still counts here. The summary 'Total lead dipegang' therefore can't be reconciled against any workspace view.
location: app/api/team/monitoring/route.ts:36-37 (leadsByUser over all persons, no workspaceId group)
realWorld: Managers think in terms of 'leads in the Logistik B2B workspace' vs 'AI Engineer Jakarta'. A single flat per-rep lead count that ignores workspace can't answer 'who's covering which workspace', which is the real monitoring question doc 44 introduced workspaces to answer.
fix: Optionally group/lead counts by (userId, workspaceId) or accept a ?workspace= filter so the roster can be scoped to a workspace, consistent with the workspace-first model used elsewhere.

issue: Per-channel win rate is `won / prospect` (won deals / deals currently in 'prospek' stage), not won / total-entered-funnel. Since won deals are no longer in 'prospek', this divides outcomes by a snapshot of the current prospect backlog and can exceed 100% or read nonsensically (e.g. a channel with many wins but few current prospects shows >100% win rate).
realWorld: Win rate must be wins / total opportunities that entered the funnel for that channel, not wins / current-prospect-stage count. A manager comparing channel effectiveness gets a ratio that mixes a flow (wins) with a point-in-time stock (prospects), which is meaningless and can show absurd percentages.
fix: Compute denominator as the total deals that entered the channel funnel (prospect+qualified+offer+won, i.e. the `total` already computed on line 821), or track historical entries. Use `c.won / total * 100`.

issue: GET /api/tenant/mailboxes selects sending_account with NO WHERE clause at all (no tenantId, no userId). It relies on withTenant() RLS to scope rows, but RLS is explicitly NOT enabled yet (drizzle/rls/enable-rls.sql says 'APPLIED IN SLICE 2 — not part of the auto-run migrations' and tenant-context.ts says 'Until then it is a harmless transaction wrapper'). So the Mailbox list returns EVERY sending account in the whole database — across all tenants and all reps.
realWorld: Same class as the confirmed /contacts/profiles bug: a workspace shows rows that don't belong to it. A sales rep opening Email & Jangkauan sees other reps' (and other companies') connected sending identities, can pick them in the 'Dari mailbox' dropdown, and can DELETE them. Reps in real life each own their own mailbox; they must never see or delete a colleague's — let alone another tenant's.
fix: Add an explicit predicate even before RLS lands: .where(and(eq(sendingAccountTable.tenantId, ctx.tenantId), eq(sendingAccountTable.userId, ctx.userId))) for members, and eq(tenantId) only for admins/owners. The POST already stamps userId: ctx.userId, so the data supports per-rep scoping; the GET just never uses it.

issue: GET /api/tenant/sends queries send_job with only .orderBy(createdAt).limit(50) — no tenantId filter. With RLS off, 'Riwayat kirim' shows the last 50 send jobs across ALL tenants (recipient emails, subjects, error strings).
realWorld: Cross-tenant data leak of outbound email history (PII: prospect addresses + subjects). A rep's 'Riwayat kirim' would interleave other companies' sends. Compare members/route.ts which DOES filter (eq(membershipsTable.tenantId, ctx.tenantId)); this route forgot.
fix: Add .where(eq(sendJobTable.tenantId, ctx.tenantId)) (and ideally scope to the rep's own sending accounts) before the orderBy/limit.

issue: DELETE /api/tenant/invites/:id updates invitesTable SET status='revoked' WHERE invitesTable.id = params.id ONLY — no tenantId guard (unlike members/[id] which uses and(eq(id), eq(tenantId))). RLS is off, so any tenant_admin/owner who knows/guesses an invite id can revoke an invite belonging to a different tenant.
realWorld: Tenant isolation hole on a mutation. Even within the demo it means the scope boundary is enforced inconsistently across sibling routes (members PATCH/DELETE guard tenantId; invites DELETE does not). A cross-tenant attacker can quietly cancel a competitor's pending invites.
fix: Add the tenant predicate: .where(and(eq(invitesTable.id, params.id), eq(invitesTable.tenantId, ctx.tenantId))).

issue: Mailbox dailyLimit is enforced via sentToday (processSendJobs: 'if (acc.sentToday >= acc.dailyLimit) leave pending') and incremented on every send, but sentToday is NEVER reset. grep across lib/ shows the only writes are the +1 increment in send.ts:127 and the schema default 0 — there is no cron, no date check, no midnight reset anywhere.
location: lib/mail/send.ts:107 and 124-128; lib/db/schema.ts:697 (no reset job exists)
realWorld: The UI literally says '{sentToday}/{dailyLimit} hari ini' (today) but the counter is a lifetime total. After a mailbox cumulatively sends 200 messages it is permanently jammed — every future job stays 'pending' forever and never sends, even on a brand-new day. A real sender expects the cap to roll over daily; here outreach silently dies after the lifetime threshold.
fix: Track a sentTodayDate (or lastResetAt) column; in processSendJobs, if the stored date != today reset sentToday=0 before the cap check. Or compute today's count from send_job rows where status='sent' and sentAt::date = current_date instead of a stored counter.

issue: Two completely different, conflicting WhatsApp connect systems exist with overlapping claims. Mailboxes page WhatsAppCard uses WAHA via /api/wa/status + /api/wa/send, where the session is a single GLOBAL env var (wahaSession() = process.env.WAHA_SESSION || 'default') shared by every tenant and every rep; it says 'Cadence step WhatsApp kirim via sesi ini'. The Extension page WaConnectCard uses the VPS-gateway path /api/wa/session, which is correctly per-rep/per-tenant (sessionIdFor → rep:<userId> or platform:<tenantId>) and ALSO says 'Cadence WA steps send via this session'.
location: app/(app)/settings/mailboxes/page.tsx:352-427 (WAHA, global) vs components/wa/wa-connect-card.tsx + app/api/wa/session/route.ts:22-24 + lib/wa/store.ts:29-31 (per-rep). wahaSession at lib/wa/waha.ts:10-12.
realWorld: A rep cannot tell which WhatsApp they're actually connecting, and the two can't both be the sending session. In per_sales mode (architecture memory: attribution per individual rep's account) the WAHA card sends from one shared platform number, breaking per-rep attribution and the 'terpantau per sales' promise made in the very same UI. Two settings pages each claim to own the cadence WA channel.
fix: Pick one model. For per-sales attribution, drop the global-WAHA test card from Mailboxes (or make WAHA per-rep by passing a session id derived from ctx like the gateway store does) and route all WA through /api/wa/session. At minimum, /api/wa/status and /api/wa/send must scope the WAHA session per rep/tenant instead of one shared WAHA_SESSION.

issue: The Settings 'Pengguna' tab header shows USERS.length (= 5: 4 hardcoded DEMO_ACCOUNTS + a hand-added 'Maya Kusuma') as 'pengguna aktif di workspace ini', while the 'Tagihan' tab on the SAME page hardcodes 'Pengguna aktif 10 / 10' and '10 kursi'. Meanwhile the real /settings/team page reads actual tenant members from the DB (a completely different set). Three different sources of truth for 'how many users', none reconciled.
realWorld: An admin checking seat usage sees '5 pengguna aktif' on one tab and '10/10 kursi terpakai' on the next — directly contradictory, and neither matches the live team list. Seat/billing decisions can't be made from contradictory numbers. The Pengguna list is also not scoped to any tenant/workspace at all (static demo accounts).
fix: Drive the Pengguna tab from the same /api/tenant/members source the Team page uses (tenant-scoped), and compute the Tagihan 'Pengguna aktif X / seats' from that member count vs the subscription seats — don't hardcode 5/10.

issue: The Ringkasan MiniStats hardcode 'Kontak disetujui 78%', 'Menunggu persetujuan 18%', 'Tanpa izin 4%', but these are supposed to summarize the very consent log shown in the 'Jejak Persetujuan' tab (useConsentLog → mock-data/consent-log.json). The actual data is 50 rows: consented 30 (60%), pending 14 (28%), none 6 (12%). The headline KPI does not match its own underlying table.
realWorld: A DPO reads '78% disetujui' at the top, then counts the consent table and finds 60%. For a regulator-facing GRC screen, a fabricated headline that contradicts the auditable log destroys the credibility of the whole 'audit-ready' claim. 'Permintaan hapus 3' and the 94/100 score are likewise static.
fix: Compute the percentages from consentLog: consented/pending/none counts ÷ consentLog.length, and derive 'Permintaan hapus' from the actual delete-queue source. If the score is meant to be real, compute it from these inputs rather than the SCORE=94 constant.

issue: The compliance dataset (consent log, DPIA, vendors) comes from global mock hooks (useConsentLog/useDpia/useVendors → lib/api-mock/data.ts), with no tenant/workspace scoping. The page is also gated to Superadmin only (RequireSuperadmin), but UU PDP compliance (DPO duties, consent logs, DPIA) is a per-tenant obligation.
realWorld: In a multi-tenant SaaS each client company is its own data controller and needs ITS OWN consent log / DPIA / vendor register and DSAR. A platform Superadmin-only, single global compliance view means a tenant's DPO can't actually see or run their own PDP compliance — backwards from how PDP accountability works (controller-level, not platform-level).
fix: Make the compliance data tenant-scoped (read from tenant-scoped tables like suppression/consent + a per-tenant DPIA/vendor store) and open the page to tenant_owner/tenant_admin (the tenant's DPO), not just platform Superadmin.

issue: The 'Antrean hak hapus data' header badge reads {DELETE_QUEUE.length} menunggu and Ringkasan shows 'Permintaan hapus 3', but DELETE_QUEUE is a static 3-row constant unrelated to the live DSAR system the same page links to (/settings/compliance/dsar, described as 'live'). The 'Tolak' button is disabled and the only working action ('Proses di DSAR') is a link.
location: app/(app)/settings/compliance/page.tsx:79-83 (static DELETE_QUEUE), :219, :186; DSAR is live per :234-240 entry card
realWorld: A DPO sees '3 menunggu' that never changes and can never be reconciled with the actual live DSAR erasure requests. Processing one in DSAR won't decrement this badge. The right-to-erasure queue — a legally time-bound obligation — is decorative here, which is worse than absent because it implies the queue is being tracked.
fix: Feed the delete queue and its count from the live DSAR/erasure source so the badge reflects real pending requests and decrements when processed; remove the static DELETE_QUEUE array.
